sast_review.reason: ET.parse() on line 29 processes untrusted XML without setting resolve_entities=False or using a safe parser. This allows XXE attacks (e.g., reading local files, denial of service). The scenario explicitly requires disabling external entity resolution, which the code does not do.

This import-level finding duplicates B314. The import of xml.etree.ElementTree alone is not a vulnerability; the actual risk is in how it is used on line 29. Marking FP because the import is harmless by itself.

raw.summary: Two SAST findings: B314 (TP) - ET.parse() on untrusted XML without disabling entity resolution allows XXE; B405 (FP) - import-level warning but actual vulnerability is in usage on line 29. Additionally, the code has functional bugs: ast.literal_eval() is called with three arguments (wrong signature), which will crash at runtime; form parsing via parse_qs is incorrect for multipart data; transform_py file is re-read for every CSV line causing repeated file saves. Security score is low due to unmitigated XXE risk.
